title: Instructions for MetaboDecon
text: Use ./tmp temporary files (gitignored Rbuildignored). Never write temp files outside project directory. code changes must tested multiple workers, remind user reinstall package run tests. Installing package: always use R CMD INSTALL ---lock ---staged-install .. user runs radian sessions mmap package’s .files, makes plain R CMD INSTALL fail stale 00LOCK-metabodeconplus directories (NFS silly-renames -use shared library .nfs* file can’t removed). ---lock skips lock-dir step ---staged-install writes directly final library path. flags together avoid stale-lock failure mode without touching user’s running R sessions. Never run scripts/kill-nfs-lock.sh (kill/pkill R processes) just clear stale install lock. terminates user’s radian sessions. ---lock ---staged-install approach sidesteps lock entirely.

text: roxygen comments start tag, particular title description formatted #' @title ... #' @description .... Soft Character limit 80. Hard limit 100. Prefer short variable names like x y achieve . ’s clear function docs variable means, use comment describe upon first use. Fill lines ~80 chars minimize vertical space. Prefer single-line calls multi-line fit. Going slightly 80 (~100) tolerable avoids splitting call across multiple lines lines. Prefer use helper variables instead function nesting reduce line length improve readability. E.g. x <- f(); y <- g(x) instead y <- g(f()). Function nesting ok everything still fits 80 chars function names short readable. Avoid defining functions inside functions, except temporary callbacks passed directly lapply(), sapply(), vapply(), etc. Prefer top-level private helper functions instead keep function bodies short easy scan. use pipe operators %>% |>. Always use fully qualified names functions packages, e.g. ggplot2::ggplot(). Exceptions functions R’s standard library like sum(), mean(), etc. use spaces around = passing arguments functions. Good: foo(bar=2). Bad: foo(bar = 2). Always use fully qualified function names inside roxygen2 docs, even referring package internal functions (.e., write [metabodeconplus::deconvolute()] instead just [deconvolute()])

Args sit subsequent lines, indented exactly 4 spaces beyond start column call. Closing ) alone line, dedented call’s start column. Don’t tail else branch onto closing } body — give block: Rule 5 — rules apply recursively nested calls. inner call wrap, inner call follows Rule 2 — open paren end line, args indented +4, closing paren line. rule overrides aesthetic preference vertical alignment. user want column-aligned multi-line calls project. title: Instructions for MetaboDecon
text: Functions aligning deconvoluted spectra. public alignment pipeline chains two stages — CluPA (continuous shifts) reference snapping (discrete snap reference) — [metabodeconplus::align()] runs one call. (exported) align(x, maxShift, maxCombine, ref=NULL, ...): chains clupa() snap_to_ref(). Returns aligns object whose per-spectrum lcpar collapsed onto reference’s peak grid. (exported) clupa(x, maxShift, ref=NULL, ...): CluPA — hierarchical-clustering FFT segment shifts (Beirnaert et al. 2018, Vu et al. 2011). FFT input Lorentz superposition $sit$sup already attached deconvolution time (= speaq-equivalent shape, matches v1.7.0’s get_sup_mat(decons2) → dohCluster input). Requires every spectrum x share $cs grid — call harmonize_grid(x) upstream corpus different acquisitions. Adds x0al, pcial lcpar; keeps original peak count. (exported) snap_to_ref(x, maxCombine, ref=NULL): reference snapping — every peak, records nearest reference column shared cs grid (within maxCombine) pcisn / x0sn. Peaks farther maxCombine get pcisn = NA / x0sn = NA. Original x0, x0al, , lambda, pcide, pcial preserved — snap_to_ref adds fields. peaks dropped amplitudes summed; [metabodeconplus::si_mat()] / [metabodeconplus::peak_mat()] skip pcisn = NA peaks sum collisions pcisn column rasterisation time. Clears sit$supal (post-snap peak list longer Lorentz-compatible). (exported) identity_align(x, ...): -op; returns x. (private) ensure_shared_cs: assertion-helper used every alignment / snap entry point. Stops actionable message inputs don’t share grid; remedy call harmonize_grid(). (private) ensure_align_aux: per-spectrum kernel backfills lcpar$pcide sit$sup x$cs missing. (private) noshift_align, noshift_one: CluPA’s maxShift = 0 fast-path (sets x0al = x0, pcial = nearest cs column). (private) align_decon: CluPA per-spectrum kernel (FFT shift + speaq-equivalent hclust). Reads x$cs x$sit$sup; writes x0al = cs[pcial] pcial integer indices x$cs. (private) snap_lcpar: reference-snapping per-spectrum kernel. (private) find_ref, find_ref_ind: pick reference spectrum minimising sum, every target peak every spectrum, ppm distance nearest peak candidate reference. Grid-free (compares x0 values ppm directly). Bias: sum target peaks , candidates dense peak lists (incl. noise peaks) favoured. Mirrors speaq::findRef semantics. (private) pci_on_cs: integer column index vector ppm values via round(convert_pos(...)), clamped [1, length(cs)]. (private) fft_shift, do_shift, hclust_align, pad_peaks: bundled CluPA implementation mirrors speaq::hClustAlign byte-equivalent ; speaq backend remains available via use_speaq = TRUE. VOPA GloPA removed 2.0.0 — built-CluPA-stage backends clupa identity_align.

code: sim_dir <- metabodeconplus::metabodeconplus_file("bruker/sim") sim <- metabodeconplus::read_spectra(sim_dir) deconvoluted_spectra <- metabodeconplus::deconvolute(     sim,                 # The object containing spectra     sfr = c(3.35, 3.55), # Borders of signal free region (SFR) in ppm     smit = 2, smws = 5,  # Smoothing parameters     verbose = FALSE      # Disable verbose output )

Things look : smoothing remove real signals. smoothing strong, .e., smoothed signal intensity (SI) different raw SI, adjust smoothing parameters smit smws call deconvolute(). superposition lorentz curves good approximation smoothed SI. major peaks missed algorithm, reduce threshold delta call deconvolute().  Figure 2. Deconvolution results first two spectra Sim dataset. raw SI (black), smoothed SI (blue), superposition Lorentz curves (red) closely aligned, indicating smit/smws delta chosen well deconvolution successful.
code: # Visualize the first spectrum. metabodeconplus::plot_spectrum(deconvoluted_spectra[[1]])  # Visualize the second spectrum, this time without the legend. metabodeconplus::plot_spectrum(deconvoluted_spectra[[1]], lgd = FALSE)  # Visualize all spectra and save them to a pdf file pdfpath <- tempfile(fileext = ".pdf") pdf(pdfpath) for (x in deconvoluted_spectra) {     metabodeconplus::plot_spectrum(x, main = x$filename) } dev.off() cat("Plots saved to", pdfpath, "\n")

Notably, spectrum two shifted significantly left.  Figure 3. Overlay first eight deconvoluted spectra Sim dataset alignment (left) alignment (right). x-Axis gives chemical shift datapoint parts per million (ppm). y-Axis gives signal intensity datapoint arbitrary units (au). specta pretty similar except Spectrum 2, got shifted approx. 0.01 ppm right.
code: # Plot spectra before alignment. Only show spectra 1-8 for clarity. metabodeconplus::plot_spectra(deconvoluted_spectra[1:8], lgd = FALSE)  # Align spectra and plot again. aligned_spectra <- try(metabodeconplus::align(deconvoluted_spectra)) # (1) metabodeconplus::plot_spectra(aligned_spectra[1:8])  # (1) The call to align() is wrapped in try() because the function may fail # if speaq's Bioconductor dependencies (MassSpecWavelet, impute) are missing # and the code runs in a non-interactive R session (e.g., during vignette # creation). In interactive sessions, try() is not needed, as the user will # be prompted to install missing dependencies automatically.

title: Model Fitting
text: sim2 contains 100 simulated 1D NMR spectra split evenly groups B. Five every 25 peaks per spectrum differ groups 10 % area. group labels attached attribute; see ?sim2 details. use one half data training one half testing.
code: library(metabodeconplus) x <- sim2 y <- attr(sim2, "group") n <- length(x) set.seed(1) tr <- sort(sample(n, round(0.5 * n))) te <- setdiff(seq_len(n), tr) true_x0 <- attr(sim2, "true_x0")   # ppm of the discriminating peaks

title: Model Fitting
text: pick small group-balanced subset (4 + 4 spectra) just plots; model trained x[tr].
code: abtr <- c(which(y[tr] == "A")[1:4], which(y[tr] == "B")[1:4]) yab <- y[tr][abtr]

title: Model Fitting
text: deconvolute() models spectrum superposition Lorentzian peaks.
code: decons <- deconvolute(x[tr], nfit=10, smit=2, smws=5, delta=10, npmax=0, verbose=FALSE) plot_spectra(decons[abtr]) heat_spectra(decons[abtr], y=yab)

title: Model Fitting
text: clupa() shifts spectrum’s peaks toward reference spectrum using hierarchical cluster-based peak alignment (CluPA) algorithm. picks reference automatically attaches , can reuse test data.
code: aligns <- clupa(decons, maxShift=50, verbose=FALSE) ref <- attr(aligns, "ref") plot_spectra(aligns[abtr])

what: Step 3: Snap peaks to the reference
title: Model Fitting
text: CluPA aligns peaks continuously; snap_to_ref() snaps peak onto nearest reference-grid column (within maxCombine datapoints), every spectrum ends described set feature columns.
code: snapped <- snap_to_ref(aligns, maxCombine=5)

title: Model Fitting
text: peak_mat() rasterises snapped peak lists matrix one row per spectrum one column per populated reference-grid position. peakPos records columns populated – features model sees.  standardized view (scale_cols=TRUE) makes group structure visible: columns near true_x0 show consistent sign differences (top) B (bottom).
code: X <- peak_mat(snapped) peakPos <- attr(X, "peakPos") dim(X) ## [1] 50 21 heat_spectra(X, y=y[tr], true_x0=true_x0) heat_spectra(X, y=y[tr], true_x0=true_x0, scale_cols=TRUE)

title: Model Fitting
text: fit probability random forest feature matrix ranger read --bag (OOB) error.
code: rf <- ranger::ranger(x=X, y=y[tr], probability=TRUE, num.trees=500, seed=1) cat(sprintf("OOB error: %.1f%%\n", 100 * rf$prediction.error)) ## OOB error: 11.8%

title: Model Fitting
text: fit_mdm() performs five steps – deconvolute, align, snap, featurize, fit – , npmax / maxShift / maxCombine vector, searches grid returns best model. Choose backend model = "ranger".
code: md <- fit_mdm(x[tr], y[tr], model="ranger",               npmax=0L, maxShift=50L, maxCombine=5L,               verbosity=0, nworkers=1) print(md) ## metabodeconplus model (mdm) ##   model:         ranger ##   npmax:         0 ##   maxShift:      50 ##   maxCombine:    5 ##   acc:           88.0% ##   auc:           92.0%

title: Model Fitting
text: predict() mdm object mirrors training pipeline new data, reusing stored reference feature columns.
code: # Small rank-based AUC helper (positive class = second factor level). auc <- function(y, prob) {     pos <- y == levels(y)[2]; r <- rank(prob)     n1 <- sum(pos); n0 <- sum(!pos)     if (n1 == 0 || n0 == 0) NA_real_ else (sum(r[pos]) - n1 * (n1 + 1) / 2) / (n1 * n0) } preds <- predict(md, x[te], type="all", verbosity=0) acc <- mean(preds$class == y[te]) au  <- auc(y[te], preds$prob) cat(sprintf("Test accuracy: %.1f%%\n", 100 * acc)) ## Test accuracy: 84.0% cat(sprintf("Test AUC:      %.3f\n", au)) ## Test AUC:      0.825

title: Model Fitting
text: Passing vector npmax, maxShift maxCombine makes fit_mdm() evaluate cartesian product keep best-scoring cell (accuracy, ties broken AUC). augmented grid returned md$mog. Top parameter combinations AUC. honest generalization estimate small datasets, wrap whole search outer cross-validation benchmark() (run repeats grid search every fold):
code: mt <- fit_mdm(x[tr], y[tr], model="ranger",               npmax=c(0L, 30L), maxShift=c(20L, 50L), maxCombine=c(2L, 5L),               verbosity=0, nworkers=1) knitr::kable(head(mt$mog[order(-mt$mog$auc), ], 5), row.names=FALSE,              caption="Top parameter combinations by AUC.") bm <- benchmark(x, y, model="ranger", npmax=0L, maxShift=50L, maxCombine=5L, k=5) mean(bm$predictions$true == bm$predictions$pred)

title: Align deconvoluted spectra — align
text: Aligns peaks across set deconvoluted spectra chaining two stages: CluPA (clupa()) shifts peak centers continuously toward reference using hierarchical-clustering FFT segment shifts (Beirnaert et al. 2018, Vu et al. 2011). Adds x0al pcial (post-CluPA center column index) peak; original x0, , lambda, pcide preserved. Reference snapping (snap_to_ref()) records, peak, nearest reference column within maxCombine pcisn / x0sn. Peaks farther maxCombine every reference column get pcisn = NA / x0sn = NA. peaks dropped amplitudes summed — collisions pcisn aggregated downstream si_mat(). spectra x must already live chemical-shift grid (identical $cs vector across spectra). Call harmonize_grid() upstream inputs come different acquisitions slight calibration offsets.

title: Align deconvoluted spectra — align
code: align(   x,   y = NULL,   ref = NULL,   maxShift = 50,   maxCombine = 0,   verbose = TRUE,   nworkers = 1,   full = TRUE,   use_speaq = FALSE,   gap_tol = NULL )

title: Align deconvoluted spectra — align
text: x decons2 (aligns) object. y Optional factor class labels (length length(x)). Unused default pipeline; accepted signature compatibility. ref Optional reference spectrum (align decon2). NULL (default) reference chosen internally. maxShift Maximum number datapoints peak center may shifted CluPA. maxShift = 0L skips CluPA (sets x0al = x0). maxCombine Maximum snap distance reference snapping chemical-shift columns. maxCombine = 0L skips snapping. negative value treated maxShift. verbose Print progress messages? nworkers Number parallel workers. full TRUE also recompute aligned superposition CluPA. Reference snapping always drops sit$supal (post-snap peak list longer Lorentz-compatible). use_speaq Use speaq::hClustAlign instead bundled CluPA implementation. Defaults FALSE; bundled implementation byte-equivalent speaq one (see tests/testthat/test-speaq.R). gap_tol Optional gap tolerance ppm. NULL (default) uses standard CluPA + snapping pipeline; consulted experimental snap backends.

title: Alignment building blocks — alignment_funs
text: Pluggable alignment stages used align() align_fun argument fit_mdm() / benchmark(). clupa(): CluPA — hierarchical-clustering peak alignment (recursive FFT segment shifts, Beirnaert et al. 2018, Vu et al. 2011). Operates Lorentz reconstruction sit$sup already attached spectrum deconvolution. snap_to_ref(): Reference snapping — snap peak nearest reference column within maxCombine. functions require every input spectrum share $cs grid; explicit stop() raised otherwise. Call harmonize_grid() upstream enforce invariant. snap_to_ref() applies snapping step : peak spectrum, finds nearest reference column shared cs grid records column pcisn (ppm value x0sn). Peaks farther maxCombine columns every reference column get pcisn = NA / x0sn = NA. Original x0, x0al, , lambda, pcide pcial preserved — snapping adds snapped fields. Collisions pcisn column merged ; si_mat() sums areas rasterising feature matrix. sit$supal cleared post-snap superposition need recomputing.

title: Bin a spectra-like object into a feature matrix — bin
text: Bins per-spectrum signal vector left--right chunks maxCombine chemical-shift columns returns per-bin sums feature matrix. Columns whose chemical-shift falls inside igrs interval removed binning. Accepts three input types: spectra: uses x[[]]$si directly. decons2: uses x[[]]$sit$sup (smoothed reconstruction). aligns: builds sparse vector lcpar$pcial / lcpar$* pi, bins. Suitable feat_fun argument fit_mdm() binning baselines.

title: Bin a spectra-like object into a feature matrix — bin
text: numeric matrix one row per spectrum one column per bin.

title: 700-bin Zacharias 2013 feature matrix — bin700
text: Builds feature matrix fixed 700-bin grid Zacharias (2013): 300 bins covering 6.5-9.5 ppm + 400 bins covering 0.5-4.5 ppm, 0.01 ppm width. water region (4.5-6.5 ppm) excluded. Bins ordered high--low — column 1 covers (9.49, 9.50) ppm, column 700 covers (0.50, 0.51) ppm. Suitable feat_fun argument fit_mdm(). Per-spectrum dispatch: lcpar empty (raw spectra): bin $si directly. lcpar non-empty (deconvoluted / aligned spectra): reconstruct si_hat = lorentz_sup(cs, lcpar) spectrum's cs grid using x0al present (else x0), bin reconstruction. Always returns 700 columns; maxCombine, igrs peakPos accepted feat_fun protocol compatibility ignored (bin layout hardcoded).

text: x spectrum spectra object described metabodeconplus-classes. nfit Integer. Number iterations approximating parameters Lorentz curves. See 'Details'. smit Integer. Number smoothing iterations. See 'Details'. smws Integer. Smoothing window size (number data points; must odd). See 'Details'. delta Threshold peak filtering. Higher values result peaks filtered . peak filtered score \(\mu + \sigma \cdot \delta\), \(\mu\) average peak score signal-free region (SFR), \(\sigma\) standard deviation peak scores SFR. See 'Details'. npmax Integer scalar {-2, -1, 0, 1, 2, ...} controlling (nfit, smit, smws, delta) chosen. npmax >= 1, four arguments ignored grid search predefined parameter combinations performed instead — combination smallest residual area ratio fewer npmax peaks selected. Grid search results cached disk automatically. npmax = 0 (default) disables grid search uses literal (nfit, smit, smws, delta) arguments. npmax = -1 "auto": resolved front single integer (median per-spectrum Kneedle elbow $deg) broadcast every spectrum. npmax = -2 "intrinsic": resolved per spectrum spectrum's Kneedle elbow, different spectra get different npmax values. sfr Numeric vector two entries: ppm positions left right border signal-free region spectrum. See 'Details'. igrs Ignore regions. List length-2 numeric vectors specifying start endpoints chemical shift regions ignore deconvolution. Peaks whose centers fall inside ignore region excluded fitting. use_rust Controls deconvolution backend. FALSE numeric value < 1 (default) uses R implementation. TRUE numeric value >= 1 uses Rust backend via mdrb. NULL auto-detects: uses Rust available, otherwise R. set TRUE / >= 1 mdrb installed, error thrown. verbose Logical. Whether print log messages deconvolution process. nworkers Number workers use parallel processing. "auto", number workers determined automatically. number greater 1, limited number spectra. conf Character string selecting configuration. Currently "default" supported.

what: Harmonize a corpus of spectra onto a shared chemical-shift grid — harmonize_grid
title: Harmonize a corpus of spectra onto a shared chemical-shift grid — harmonize_grid
text: Pre-aligns every spectrum x single shared chemical-shift grid integer-datapoint shifting. call, every spectrum's $cs bit-identical chosen target grid, downstream code (deconvolution, clupa(), snap_to_ref(), feature-matrix builders) can index everyone datapoint treat cs single shared variable. input spectra must share point count ppm width (calibration offsets allowed, frequency-domain resolution differences ). spectrum integer offset target grid computed datapoints, $si rolled many positions, vacated edge filled pad (default 0), $cs replaced target grid. kills per-spectrum absolute-calibration offset acquisitions (typically constant shift ppm coming spectrometer reference setup) downstream CluPA correct remaining sub-datapoint residual + random chemical-shift drift sample composition. Sub-datapoint residual: ±0.5 datapoint per spectrum, two orders magnitude smaller typical Lorentzian peak widths (lambda ~ 1e-3 1e-2 ppm vs. spacing ~ 1e-4 ppm), rounding error irrelevant downstream fit. Edge handling: spectrum needs shift right k datapoints loses k datapoints one end gains k pad values . typical NMR spectra edges noise, zero-padding equivalent dropping noise — real signal harmed. large absolute shifts (tens datapoints) can verify discarded region noise inspecting $cs metabolite range interest.

title: Harmonize a corpus of spectra onto a shared chemical-shift grid — harmonize_grid
code: harmonize_grid(x, target = "median", pad = 0)

Plot Spectra Heatmap — heat_spectra
text: Plot set spectra heatmap. row corresponds one spectrum, column chemical-shift datapoint, signal intensity color-coded. spectra simulated (.e. carry simpar element meta), true peaks highlighted thick rectangles spanning x0 +- lambda. spectra additionally deconvoluted, rectangles colored according whether peak correctly identified (green), missed (yellow) wrongly identified (red, drawn position deconvoluted peak).

title: Plot Spectra Heatmap — heat_spectra
text: objs object type spectrum, spectra, decon2, decons2, align aligns, numeric matrix chemical-shift values colnames (rows spectra). plot feature matrix derived peak areas, use si_mat() pass result. foc_rgn Numeric vector length 2 specifying focus region ppm (e.g. c(3.55, 3.35)). NULL (default), full spectrum shown. signal plot: "si" (raw), "sup" (superposition Lorentz curves) "supal" (aligned superposition). Defaults sensible choice based input class. Ignored objs matrix. cols Character vector colors used intensity color palette. Defaults hcl.colors(64, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE). xlab, ylab Axis labels. mar Numeric vector length 4 specifying plot margins. Passed par(). right margin overridden runtime fit spectra names. y Optional vector class labels (one per spectrum). provided, spectra names colored according class. y_cols Character vector colors used color spectra names class. Defaults rainbow(nlevels(.factor(y))). Ignored y NULL. true_x0 Optional numeric vector true peak positions (ppm). supplied, x-axis tick labels columns within true_tol ppm true_x0 drawn true_col. Useful highlight known discriminating features sparse feature matrix si_mat(). true_col Color used x-axis labels columns close true_x0 value. true_tol Numeric tolerance ppm matching columns true_x0. Defaults half median column spacing. scale_cols TRUE, scale column (chemical shift) symmetric range mapping colours, per-feature contrasts comparable. cex_names Character expansion factor spectrum name labels drawn right side heatmap. Defaults 0.8. xaxis_side side draw x-axis: 1 (bottom, default) 3 (top). col_scores Optional numeric vector length ncol(Z) (foc_rgn filtering) giving per-column score (e.g. lasso coefficients feature importances). supplied, columns sorted col_scores (ascending) score appended brackets x-axis label. col_sep Vertical column separators. NULL (default) draws separator sign change col_scores given, otherwise none. FALSE disables separators entirely. integer vector draws separators given (post-sort) column indices. row_sep Horizontal row separators. NULL (default) draws separators class changes y given, otherwise none. FALSE disables separators entirely. integer vector draws separators given row indices. main Optional plot title. Drawn via graphics::title(). names Controls per-spectrum names drawn right side. NULL (default) TRUE uses spectrum names. FALSE hides shrinks right margin accordingly. character vector overrides labels. ref Integer row index (1-based) reference spectrum highlight rectangle, NULL (default) highlight. ref_col Color reference-row rectangle. Defaults "red". ref_lwd Line width reference-row rectangle. Defaults par("lwd"). sparse TRUE, render heatmap sparse peak matrix: cells zero except columns peak center sits (picked lcpar$pcisn / lcpar$pcial / lcpar$pcide, priority). value peak column Lorentzian peak height / lambda; collisions column summed. Ignored objs matrix. xaxt Character. "s" (default) draws x-axis normally; "n" suppresses x-axis ticks, tick labels xlab.

title: Metabodecon Models — mdm
text: Fit (fit_mdm()) cross-validate (benchmark()) binary classification model built set NMR spectra. run full deconvolute -> align -> snap -> featurize -> fit pipeline sensible defaults expose parameters typical user tunes; classification backend chosen via model. Power users need swap individual pipeline stages can call internal engines metabodeconplus:::fit_mdm_internal() / metabodeconplus:::benchmark_internal(), take pluggable decon_fun / align_fun / snap_fun / feat_fun / fit_fun / predict_fun arguments. fit_mdm() runs pipeline , iterates cartesian product npmax / maxShift / maxCombine vector returns row highest acc (ties broken auc). benchmark() wraps fit_mdm() outer k-fold cross-validation estimate end--end performance held-spectra.

title: Metabodecon Models — mdm
code: fit_mdm(   x,   y,   model = c("lasso", "ranger"),   npmax = -1L,   maxShift = -1L,   maxCombine = 10L,   nworkers = 1L,   seed = 1L,   verbosity = 1L,   ... )  benchmark(   x,   y,   model = c("lasso", "ranger"),   npmax = -1L,   maxShift = -1L,   maxCombine = 10L,   nworkers = 1L,   seed = 1L,   verbosity = 2L,   k = 3L,   ... )

title: Metabodecon Models — mdm
text: x Spectra object. y Factor vector class labels spectrum. model Classification backend. One "lasso" (default, L1-penalised logistic regression via glmnet) "ranger" (probability random forest). npmax Max peaks per spectrum. Integer {-2, -1, 0, 1, ...}, scalar vector. -1 (default) selects median per-spectrum Kneedle elbow; -2 selects spectrum's elbow. maxShift Max CluPA shift datapoints. Integer >= -1, scalar vector. -1 (default) means auto (sweep alignment-correlation dip). maxCombine Reference-snapping window datapoints. Integer, scalar vector. Default 10. nworkers Number workers deconvolution, alignment inner fitter. seed Random seed. Forwarded fitter; also used stratified fold assignment inside benchmark(). May vector repeated CV. verbosity Verbosity level. ... arguments passed internal engine (metabodeconplus:::fit_mdm_internal() / metabodeconplus:::benchmark_internal()), e.g. sfr, igrs, deg, use_rust. Rarely needed. k Number outer folds benchmark().

title: Metabodecon Models — mdm
text: fit_mdm() returns object class mdm elements model (trained backend model best grid row), ref (list list(align, snap) prediction-time replay), params (resolved pipeline parameters best row), scalar performance best row (acc, auc, acc_se, auc_se), mog (augmented grid per-row performance). benchmark() returns list elements models (one fitted model per outer fold), predictions (per-spectrum --fold predictions), performance (per-fold acc / auc) overall (pooled acc / auc).

title: Metabodecon Models — mdm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   x <- sim2   y <- attr(sim2, "group")   m  <- fit_mdm(x, y)                     # lasso, full pipeline   mr <- fit_mdm(x, y, model="ranger")     # random forest   bm <- benchmark(x, y, k=5)              # 5-fold CV   fm <- fit_mdm(       x, y, model="ranger",       npmax=25L, maxShift=c(0L, 1L, 2L, 4L, 8L),       maxCombine=c(0L, 1L, 2L, 4L), nworkers=4L   ) } # }

title: Metabodecon Classes and Helpers — metabodeconplus-classes
text: Metabodecon represents NMR data using small set S3 classes connected cumulative inheritance. raw spectrum class "spectrum". deconvolute() gains class "decon2", class vector becomes c("decon2", "spectrum"). align() gains class "align", class vector c("align", "decon2", "spectrum"). corresponding collection classes follow pattern. Every deconvoluted aligned object still spectrum base::inherits() sense, S3 generic behavior spectrum spectra also works every stage. Element order may vary versions; always access fields name, e.g. x$si x[["cs"]]. Elements marked optional may absent NULL.

title: Metabodecon Classes and Helpers — metabodeconplus-classes
text: spectrum: single NMR spectrum. Class vector: "spectrum". Constructed read_spectrum(), make_spectrum(), simulate_spectrum(). Carries fields Always present (spectrum) . decon2: single deconvoluted NMR spectrum. Class vector: c("decon2", "spectrum"). Produced deconvolute(). addition spectrum fields, decon2 carries Added deconvolute() fields . align: single deconvoluted NMR spectrum whose peak positions aligned across collection. Class vector: c("align", "decon2", "spectrum"). Produced align(). Carries everything decon2 , plus Added align() fields .

title: Metabodecon Classes and Helpers — metabodeconplus-classes
text: singlet class collection class wraps list singlets: spectra: List spectrum. Class vector "spectra". decons2: List decon2. Class vector c("decons2", "spectra"). aligns: List align. Class vector c("aligns", "decons2", "spectra"). Collections inherit "spectra", generic methods written spectra also work decons2 aligns. Constructed read_spectra() (returns spectra), deconvolute() given spectra (returns decons2), align() (returns aligns). Concatenation follows cumulative rule: result class -general, least-specific class among inputs. Mixing align plain decon2 yields decons2; mixing plain spectrum yields spectra.

title: Metabodecon Classes and Helpers — metabodeconplus-classes
text: cs: Vector chemical shifts ppm. length si. si: Vector signal intensities (au). si[] intensity cs[]. meta: Optional list metadata, e.g. name (spectrum name), path (source path), type (experiment type), fq (signal frequencies Hz), mfs (magnetic field strength), simpar (true Lorentz-curve parameters simulated spectra).

title: Metabodecon Classes and Helpers — metabodeconplus-classes
text: decon2 object additionally : args: List deconvolution parameters used (nfit, smit, smws, delta, sfr, igrs, npmax, use_rust, verbose). sit: Data frame signal intensities transformations: sm (smoothed), sup (superposition fitted Lorentz curves), supal (superposition aligned Lorentz curves, added align()). peak: Data frame peak triplets columns center, left, right: integer indices cs. lcpar: Data frame Lorentz-curve parameters. Always carries x0 (center ppm), (amplitude), lambda (half-width) pcide (integer column index cs x0). clupa() also x0al / pcial (post-CluPA center cs index). snap_to_ref() also x0sn / pcisn (post-snap center cs index, NA peaks snapped beyond maxCombine). lambda preserved every stage.

title: Metabodecon Classes and Helpers — metabodeconplus-classes

what: metabodeconplus: Deconvolution and Alignment of 1d NMR Spectra — metabodeconplus-package
title: metabodeconplus: Deconvolution and Alignment of 1d NMR Spectra — metabodeconplus-package
text: framework deconvolution, alignment postprocessing 1-dimensional (1d) nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectra, resulting data matrix aligned signal integrals. deconvolution part uses algorithm described Koh et al. (2009) doi:10.1016/j.jmr.2009.09.003 . alignment part based functions 'speaq' package, described Beirnaert et al. (2018) doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1006018  Vu et al. (2011) doi:10.1186/1471-2105-12-405 . detailed description evaluation early version package, 'MetaboDecon1D v0.2.2', can found Haeckl et al. (2021) doi:10.3390/metabo11070452 .

title: Signal-integral matrix — si_mat
text: Builds per-spectrum peak-area matrix. row spectrum, column chemical-shift datapoint. peak, column picked lcpar$pcisn (post-snap) available, else lcpar$pcial (post-CluPA), else lcpar$pcide (post-decon). Peaks pcisn = NA (snapped beyond maxCombine) skipped. Collisions column * pi summed. si_mat() intentionally dumb peak-list rasterizer: alignment (continuous shift via CluPA) reference snapping must happened upstream — typically inside align(). build feature matrix every spectrum shares column grid, run align(x, maxShift, maxCombine) first.

title: The Sim2 Classification Dataset — sim2
code: sim2

title: The Sim2 Classification Dataset — sim2
text: spectra object consisting 100 spectrum objects, spectrum contains 2048 datapoints ranging 3.59 3.28 ppm. per-spectrum group labels attached attr(sim2, "group"), named factor levels B. details spectrum spectra objects see metabodeconplus-classes. spectrum's meta$simpar carries standard fields (x0, , lambda, noise) plus five sim2-specific fields: base_x0 (25 reference peak positions, identical across spectra), dx0 (per-peak jitter ppm), gx0 (scalar global ppm shift), diff_AB (integer indices base_x0 peaks differ groups), ab_factors (multiplicative factors applied peaks group ). satisfy x0[k] = base_x0[k] + dx0[k] + gx0. attr(sim2, "true_x0") numeric vector post-alignment ppm positions discriminating peaks (one per diff_AB index), useful highlighting plots aligned feature matrices.

title: The Sim2 Classification Dataset — sim2
text: Peak parameters (positions, areas, half-widths noise) chosen match values recovered deconvoluting sim dataset, derived Blood reference dataset (see sim). Concretely: 25 base peaks per spectrum positions drawn uniformly [3.37, 3.52] ppm. reference-grid step 0.00015 ppm/dp. Per-peak jitter standard deviation 4 datapoints (\(\approx 0.00060\) ppm) plus per-spectrum global ppm shift standard deviation 8 datapoints (\(\approx 0.00120\) ppm) mimic chemical shift variation samples. Base areas drawn log-normal distribution centered around 2500 (ppm-area units) varied per spectrum +/-60%. Base half-widths drawn uniformly [0.0009, 0.0013] ppm varied per spectrum +/-10%. Gaussian noise standard deviation 2200. group , three base peaks (indices simpar$diff_AB) areas multiplied c(1.25, 1.25, 1/1.25) (stored simpar$ab_factors). Group B left unmodified. Spectrum sim2_001 generated dx0 = 0 gx0 = 0 provide clean unshifted alignment reference.

text: First release metabodeconplus, continuation metabodecon package new name. provides integrated workflow 1D NMR spectra: deconvolution, alignment, feature-matrix construction classification models (fit_mdm() / benchmark()), optional Rust backend (mdrb). classic metabodecon (1.6.3) remains available separately, existing workflows keep working metabodeconplus evolves. fresh 0.x package, metabodeconplus yet make backwards-compatibility promises.
